partial_detail: Reverse-applied only the two loan_types.py defects (2 of 5); the 2 loan f2p pass but the 3 phone f2p still fail -> required passed 14/17 -> reward 0.
base_defects_visible_suite: IDENTICAL to clean HEAD: 7 failed / 166 passed / 24 skipped. The 7 failures are the pre-existing rot set (test_inbound_calls x3, test_document_ingestion_readiness x2, test_document_report x1, test_ingestion_stale_recovery x1) and are byte-for-byte the same set at base+defects; no visible test changed verdict.
env_note: No task-specific env required. phone.py + loan_types.py are pure (phone functions given the phonenumbers library, loan-type resolvers are pure table lookups); the smbcontacts/cartesia p2p suites are deterministic and offline. The JWT_SECRET_KEY baked into loangenus-repo:v1 is sufficient; no pydantic Settings ValidationError.

defects.wrong_output: +12079460958 (US wins because the GB default is no longer prepended to the fallback list)
defects.why_no_visible_test_catches: Every visible caller uses default_region=US, which is already first in _CRM_FALLBACK_REGIONS, so dropping the prepend changes nothing; only a non-US default loses its priority.
defects.derivability: DERIVABLE. The module comment (line 18: 'Regions tried when no + country code is provided (default region first)') and the try_normalize docstring ('Tries the configured default region first, then common regions') both state default-region-first. `if not default:` never prepends the default (default is always truthy after `(default_region or _FALLBACK_REGION).strip().upper()`), so the region list no longer honors the documented order. Uniquely-correct side = `if default:`. Pinned by the docstring + comment, not a free convention pick.

defects.wrong_output: +491201234567 (last fallback region DE instead of the earliest region US)
defects.why_no_visible_test_catches: The possible-match return is only reached when NO region yields a valid number; every visible input resolves via a valid match (8050306043 -> IN) or the explicit-+ branch (+91...), so this line is never exercised.
defects.derivability: DERIVABLE (easy). The sibling line two above returns `valid_matches[0]` (clean, untouched), and the docstring/comment pin default-region-first, so the parallel possible-match branch must also return index [0]; the visible `[0]` vs `[-1]` asymmetry between two structurally identical lines is the tell. Uniquely-correct side = `possible_matches[0]`. NOT a nothing-pins tie-break: the adjacent valid-match line and the documented ordering both fix which region wins.

defects.wrong_output: raises ValueError (a stray leading 0 after + -> unparseable country code)
defects.why_no_visible_test_catches: No visible test feeds a 00-prefixed number to the strict normalizer; all visible strict inputs are US numbers dialed without the 00 access code.
defects.derivability: DERIVABLE (easy). `00` is the ITU international call prefix, equivalent to `+` (strip both zeros) — a domain standard the code's own comment invokes ('Allow 00 international prefix (common outside US)'). The twin block in try_normalize_phone_to_e164 (line 61, clean) uses `value[2:]`, pinning the correct slice; `value[1:]` leaves a leading zero (`+0044...`) that phonenumbers cannot parse. Uniquely-correct side = `value[2:]`.

defects.wrong_output: Hard money (only the first word capitalized)
defects.why_no_visible_test_catches: The 20 catalog ids all return their exact label from the loop; the .title() fallback is only reached for an id no longer in the catalog, and .title()/.capitalize() differ only on multi-word ids. No visible test asserts loan_type_label on an unknown multi-word id.
defects.derivability: DERIVABLE. Every LOAN_TYPE_CHOICES label is Title Case ('Term Loan', 'Working Capital Loan', 'Bridge Loan', ...), so the humanization fallback for an off-catalog id must match that convention (each word capitalized). `.capitalize()` produces sentence case (first word only), inconsistent with all 20 visible labels. Uniquely-correct side = `.title()` (or any equivalent word-by-word capitalization; the gold test asserts the value, so alternatives pass). Pinned by the visible catalog labels, not a free style pick.

defects.wrong_output: Working_Capital (raw casing echoed instead of the stored lowercase id)
defects.why_no_visible_test_catches: This branch is only reached by an underscore-bearing id typed with its underscore (compact single-token ids go through the earlier compact branch), and only differs when that id carries uppercase. Mixed-case-with-underscore is a form no visible test or casual user feeds.
defects.derivability: DERIVABLE. The docstring says 'Map a CSV/UI loan type string to a canonical id'; the catalog ids are all lowercase; and every other return branch yields a lowercase canonical id (`return compact` where compact is `value.lower()...`, the alias lookup returns a lowercase id, `return choice["id"]` returns a lowercase id). `return value` echoes the raw casing, breaking the canonical-id contract every sibling branch upholds. Uniquely-correct side = `return value.lower()`.

leak_checks: Planted defects invisible to git: after planting, environment/Dockerfile runs `rm -rf .git && git init -qb main && ... && git commit -qm "import codebase"`; verified in-image `git log --oneline | wc -l` == 1 and `git diff` empty. Gold file test_phone_alias_boundaries.py is absent from the image and enters only via config.json test_patch at grade time. The instruction names the two modules and the symptom class but no function names, no example inputs/aliases, no boundary direction, and no defect count. Avoids the two sites the sibling forge-contacts-invites task used (the _CRM_FALLBACK_REGIONS 'IN' entry and the resolve_loan_type label-loop id/label return), both of which are exercised by visible tests and would be observable.
fairness_audit: All five gold tests are pure direct calls with ZERO mocks, asserting the correct output value rather than any implementation detail; alternative correct fixes (e.g. a manual word-by-word title-caser for loan_type_label, or an explicit default-region-first ordering) were spot-checked and also satisfy the assertions, so no oracle implementation choice is encoded. All five defected functions are live code reachable from smbcontacts/service.py, smbcontacts/schemas.py, smbinvites/{service,schemas}.py and calls/{inbound,service}.py. Every gold test references only public functions that exist at base. The full existing suite is byte-for-byte identical (7F/166P/24S) at base+defects, so there is no run-pytest-see-red gradient pointing at any defect; the agent must read and reason about the edge/boundary logic in the two named files to locate all five single-token slips.
derivability_audit: All 5 defects pass the binding derivability audit: a strong engineer reading only the visible code, docstrings, and domain standards can determine the uniquely-correct side of each boundary. ZERO convention-picks — no unpinned band-edge >=/> and no nothing-pins tie-break; every boundary is fixed by a visible artifact. Target mix achieved: 2 easy-derivable + 3 medium-derivable. Easy (standard/sibling makes the correct side obvious once the line is found): #2 possible-match tiebreak (pinned by the adjacent clean `valid_matches[0]` line + the '[0] vs [-1]' asymmetry) and #3 00-prefix (pinned by the ITU 00->+ standard the comment invokes AND the `value[2:]` twin in the sibling function). Medium (derivable but requires tracing the resolution flow to confirm): #1 default-region priority (pinned by the 'default region first' docstring + comment, but the reader must realize `not default` is always false), #4 loan_type_label Title Case (pinned by the LOAN_TYPE_CHOICES labels, but the reader must reach the off-catalog fallback and compare), #5 resolve_loan_type lowercase-canonical (pinned by the 'canonical id' docstring + every sibling return branch, but the reader must confirm which branch fires and that raw casing violates the contract). No defect sits at p~=0 (context-free convention pick) or the whole-task-trivializing p~=1 (context-free constant like the E.164 15-max, which was dropped anyway as unreachable).
resistance_assessment: Targeting Sonnet 0/5, Opus ~1-3/10. A solve requires finding ALL FIVE edge-only single-token slips with the whole suite green (no failing local test as a signpost). Three are subtle region/format edges in try_/strict phone normalization that only bite on non-US defaults, no-valid-region ties, and the 00 access prefix; two are lookup edges that only bite legacy ids and mixed-case underscore ids. The only residual discoverability tell is the cross-function inconsistency that normalize_phone_to_e164 slices value[1:] for the 00 prefix while the twin in try_normalize_phone_to_e164 still slices value[2:] (analogous to the canonical task's tenant-concentration >= vs > sibling inconsistency); nothing in any test signposts it.
